codeReview.summary: The legacy AES-128-CBC PEM parsing work is in place, but the unsaved-passphrase path is not fully truthful: choosing not to persist the passphrase marks the identity as BLOCKED and shows a repair message claiming Keystore invalidation, even though nothing was invalidated.

issues.description: When an encrypted key is imported without saving its passphrase, this branch forces `passphraseStorageState` to `SecretStorageState.BLOCKED`. That makes the library row hit the generic repair path at lines 277-281 and show `Repair needed: Keystore access changed or the saved secret was invalidated`, which is false for a brand-new identity where the user explicitly chose not to save the passphrase. This violates the feature/validation requirement that the saved-vs-unsaved result be visible and truthful in the identity library/details.

summary: Fail: the feature correctly adds legacy encrypted PEM parsing and retry coverage, but the unsaved-passphrase outcome is mislabeled as a repaired/invalidated-secret state instead of a truthful intentionally-unsaved state.
